title: Reinforcement Learning & RLHF — PPO, GRPO & Preference Optimization
description: Reinforcement Learning covers MDP foundations, Q-learning, policy gradients, and advanced LLM alignment protocols. Understand PPO math, GRPO (group relative) objectives, DPO derivations, process reward models (PRM), and reward hacking mitigations.

sections.title: MDP & Bellman Equations
sections.description: psychology Intuition: Markov Decision Processes define the RL setting. State transitions depend only on current state and action. Dynamic programming resolves the Bellman equations sequentially.

sections.title: PPO: Clipped Actor-Critic Loss for LLMs
sections.description:
psychology Intuition: Proximal Policy Optimization prevents catastrophic updates. It uses a **clipped surrogate objective** comparing new and old policy distributions, pessimism bounds, and Generalized Advantage Estimation (GAE).

bar_chart PPO loss: $L^{CLIP}(\theta) = \mathbb{E}[\min(r_t(\theta)A_t, \text{clip}(r_t(\theta), 1-\epsilon, 1+\epsilon)A_t)]$. $\epsilon \approx 0.2$.

sections.title: GRPO: Group Relative Policy Optimization
sections.description:
psychology Intuition: **GRPO** (used in DeepSeek models) eliminates the critic model to save memory. Instead of using a value network to estimate state advantage, GRPO samples a group of $G$ outputs for each prompt, scores them with the reward model, and calculates advantages by normalizing the scores within the group.

bar_chart Advantage: $A_i = \frac{R_i - \text{mean}(R)}{\text{std}(R)}$. This ensures zero-sum reward updates within the group, stabilizing policy gradients without a critic.
sections.codeBlock:
def calculate_grpo_advantages(rewards):
    # rewards: tensor of shape (Group_Size,)
    mean = rewards.mean()
    std = rewards.std() + 1e-8
    return (rewards - mean) / std

sections.title: DPO vs. IPO vs. ORPO: Math & Tradeoffs
sections.description: psychology Intuition: **Direct Preference Optimization (DPO)** bypasses reward model training and policy search by optimizing SFT weights directly. **IPO (Identity PO)** regularizes the objective to prevent over-fitting. **ORPO (Odds Ratio PO)** fuses SFT and alignment into a single loss, removing reference model memory overhead entirely.

sections.title: Reward Modeling: Bradley-Terry & PRMs
sections.description: psychology Intuition: Reward models estimate response quality. **Outcome Reward Models (ORMs)** evaluate the final text block. **Process Reward Models (PRMs)** grade every step of reasoning sequentially, which is critical for complex math and logical reasoning tasks (e.g. o1 style models).

details: Staff Engineer Summary: Aligning models requires selecting between direct and reinforcement paradigms. Use DPO/ORPO for simplicity and memory savings. For reasoning models with verifiers, use GRPO to sample groups, normalize advantages, and apply step-level grading via Process Reward Models (PRMs).
